recipes.title: Slow Cooker BBQ Venison Backstrap
recipes.subtitle: Camp Recipe
recipes.description: Tender, shredded BBQ venison that is perfect for sandwiches after a long day in the woods.
ingredients: 1–2 lbs venison backstrap or venison roast
1 cup BBQ sauce, plus more for finishing
steps: Season the venison with BBQ rub, salt, and pepper.
Optional: sear in a hot pan for extra flavor before slow cooking.
Place venison in the slow cooker with BBQ sauce, beef broth, onion, garlic, Worcestershire sauce, and apple cider vinegar.
Cook on LOW for 6–8 hours until the meat is tender and falls apart easily.
Shred the meat with two forks.
Stir in extra BBQ sauce before serving.
tips: Venison is lean, so the broth and sauce help keep it tender.
Low and slow cooking is the key to pull-apart texture.
This is even better the next day around the fire.

recipes.title: Slow Cooker Venison Chili
recipes.subtitle: Camp Recipe
recipes.description: A hearty, lean venison chili slow-cooked with beans, tomatoes, onion, garlic, and warm spices.
steps: Brown the ground venison in a skillet and drain excess liquid if needed.
Add the venison to the slow cooker.
Add onion, garlic, diced tomatoes, tomato sauce, beans, chili powder, cumin, salt, pepper, and broth.
Taste and adjust seasoning before serving.
tips: Browning the venison first gives the chili deeper flavor.
Slow cooking helps tenderize the meat and blend the spices.
This recipe is even better the next day.

recipes.title: Swamp Stew
recipes.subtitle: Camp Recipe
recipes.description: A hearty one-pot stew with smoked sausage, greens, beans, and a little kick—perfect for camp nights.
ingredients: 1 can turnip greens or fresh chopped greens
1 can Rotel tomatoes with green chilies
steps: Brown the sliced smoked sausage in a large pot or Dutch oven.
Add chopped onion and cook until softened.
Stir in turnip greens, black-eyed peas, and Rotel tomatoes.
Pour in chicken broth and mix well.
serving: Serve over rice for a heartier meal
Add hot sauce for extra kick
tips: Smoked sausage provides most of the flavor—use a good one.
Let it simmer longer for deeper flavor.
Tastes even better reheated the next day.
